posts.text: Two oil tankers and two LPG carriers are crossing the Strait of Hormuz **inbound** via the Omani route with their AIS turned on (under heavy US Navy / Airforce watch). Washington trying hard to keep the Omani route on.
posts.summary: The U.S. is actively monitoring shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz via an alternative Omani route to maintain oil and LPG supply flow, signaling efforts to keep critical energy exports moving through the region despite potential security concerns.

posts.text: Ok I have a cool hack for making your 4th of July burgers incredibly juicy. Mix 1/4 tsp. of baking soda with 1 TBS. of water. Make a slurry and mix per pound of ground beef. Thank me later.

posts.text:
Iran’s foreign affairs minister:

“… Any attempt to adopt new or separate arrangements compared to what is underway by the Islamic Republic of Iran, will only lead to more complicated situations and delays in the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and will increase the tensions,
posts.summary: Iran's foreign minister is warning that alternative arrangements to current Iranian proposals regarding the Strait of Hormuz could delay its reopening and escalate tensions, signaling potential disruption to one of the world's most critical oil chokepoints. The statement suggests Iran may obstruct strait access if its preferred terms are not accepted, creating supply risk for global oil markets.
